summary_en: Final dividend for FY3/26 raised from 40 to 45 yen/share (+12.5%); combined with interim dividend of 40 yen/share already paid, annual dividend revised to 85 yen/share from prior 80 yen forecast. Rationale: balanced shareholder returns against internal reserves and business reinvestment needs.
title_en: Notice of Revision to FY3/26 Dividend Forecast (Dividend Increase)
translation_en_brief: On 14 May 2026, Hibino Corporation's board of directors approved an upward revision to the final dividend forecast for the fiscal year ending 31 March 2026. The final dividend per share has been raised from the previously forecasted 40 yen to 45 yen per share, representing a 12.5% increase. Combined with the interim dividend of 40 yen per share already paid in the second quarter, the full-year dividend is now forecast at 85 yen per share, compared to the prior guidance of 80 yen per share. The company stated that the increase reflects a comprehensive evaluation of current-period performance, future business prospects, and changes in the operating environment, balanced against the need to maintain adequate internal reserves for business development. The final dividend remains subject to board approval scheduled for 22 May 2026.
